concept: Discriminate / Impose Disadvantage Through a Perceived Category
description: To treat a person or group disadvantageously through an actual, assigned, or perceived trait or category. The act may work through a rule that appears general while its burden falls unevenly. 'Pharomu' (exclude) names being kept out; 'wheparu' identifies the category-shaped disadvantage behind that or another harm.
sound_symbolism: Breathy 'whe' begins with a split in ordinary relation, and firm 'pa' sets the imposed category between people. The unequal treatment carries into action and consequence through 'ru'.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb: 'shia mia wheparu.' (They discriminate against me.) The basis, actor, practice, and effect can be stated in surrounding clauses. The event-noun rule supplies discrimination; exact legal definitions remain source material where jurisdiction matters.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A cooperative institution needs language for the rule that distributes harm unevenly, including when its designers called the rule neutral.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The word lets a speaker connect unequal treatment to the category used against them instead of reducing the event to private dislike.

tags.cognition: an assigned or perceived category directing action
tags.community: unequal treatment of a person or group
